response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web software developer with strengths in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the role requires deep hardware expertise: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. Nothing in the resume demonstrates experience with silicon architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, or SIMD/MIMD systems. The low-latency DLang work and brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments are the closest touchpoints, but they fall far short of principal-level performance architecture for AI hardware.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers) handling millions of requests per second
Long, consistent engineering career (18+ years) with BS in Computer Science, meeting the formal education/experience bar
Some C/C++ and embedded/hardware-adjacent exposure (National Instruments, robotic arms, audio DSP thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator experience whatsoever
No background in deep neural network training/inference optimization or hardware-software co-design
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not hardware performance architecture at principal level

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and AI accelerator expertise
No deep-learning workload or GPU performance background
